Description:
Premium roofing solution featuring Owens Corning Duration shingles with vibrant Designer color options. This system delivers commercial-grade durability built for the toughest Western weather and is backed by our Lifetime No-Leak Guarantee and 50-Year Material Warranty. It is engineered for lasting value across homes, businesses, and multifamily properties.

RHIVE CORE COMMITMENT & GUARANTEE
LIABILITY INSURANCE:
$2M General Aggregate, $1M Personal & ADV Injury This policy meets all state and contractual requirements, providing robust coverage for property protection during all project phases.

  
WORKERS COMPENSATION:
Certified RHIVE workers with full workers' compensation coverage. Safety is non-negotiable

LICENSE & CERTIFICATES:
RHIVE maintains all required state and manufacturer licensing and certifications for valid system installation and warranties.

BUILDING PERMIT:
Complete permit management for every full roof project, secured and managed by RHIVE, unless otherwise specified in the scope of work (e.g., government contracts, prepaid permitting, etc.).

PROJECT MANAGER:
Dedicated Project Manager overseeing all site logistics, certified quality inspections, comprehensive cleanup, and required project documentation.

INSTALLATION WARRANTY:
RHIVE Installer No-Leak Guarantee - covers all roof repairs for leaks caused by improper installation.

MANUFACTURER'S WARRANTY:
Owens Corning Preferred Protection (Total System): Includes 50 years of non-prorated material and labor coverage for manufacturing defects, 10 years of workmanship coverage, 130 MPH wind resistance, and 25 years of algae resistance. This superior warranty is included at no extra cost, conferred automatically by RHIVE's certified installation of the Owens Corning Total Protection System.



DUMPSTER & WASTE MANAGEMENT:
Cost included for disposal of all estimated layers, debris, and waste. Includes placement and management of dumpster and ground tarps.

TEAR-OFF:
Full removal of all existing roofing material down to the decking. Any additional layers will be photographed and communicated to the homeowner for unmatched transparency.

DECKING INSPECTION:
Certification inspection of all roof surface decking. Non-compliant wood quoted with homeowner approval.

DECKING:
Up to 100 sq ft of 7/16 OSB Decking replacement included.

CAP NAILS:
Water-sealing, electro-galvanized ring shank cap nails installed to securely adhere underlayment against high winds.

COIL NAILS:
Electro-galvanized coil nails installed with 6 nails per shingle (utilizing SureNail Technology) for superior wind resistance.

DRIP METAL:
28 G Steel drip edge installed on the entire perimeter per manufacturer specifications for clean water diversion.

ICE & WATER SHIELD:
Owens Corning WeatherLock® waterproofing membrane installed in all leak-prone areas (eaves and valleys). Installation meets IRC code (24" past warm wall) and includes a RHIVE minimum of 6 feet of coverage at all eaves for superior, double-layer protection.

UNDERLAYMENT:
Owens Corning ProArmor: A high-performance synthetic underlayment installed on the entire non-covered roof field as a secondary water-shedding barrier per system specifications.

STARTER STRIP SHINGLES:
Owens Corning Starter Strip Plus: A specialized shingle installed on all eaves for a straight edge and an effective perimeter seal against high winds.

FIELD SHINGLES:
Owens Corning Duration Series Shingles: 50-Year Preferred Protection Shingles featuring SureNail Technology, 130 MPH Wind Rating, and guaranteed StreakGuard™ Algae Resistance 25YR. Includes all standard and Designer color options.


PIPE FLASHINGS:
Full Metal Pipe Jacks for lifetime longevity - all jackets replaced, sealed, and painted.

FLASHING:
28 G Steel used for all penetrations: L-Metal for headwalls, and Step Flashing (woven) to meet and often exceed IRC height/lap requirements at sidewalls. New flashing is 4 in×4 in×8 in to maximize coverage.

VENTILATION:
Balanced Ventsure System: Exhaust uses Sky Runner LTE or 4-Foot Strip Ridge Vent (for high NFVA). Intake is via soffit or deck-mount air for non-soffit roofs. (Turbines used only for hipped roofs; all turtle vents are permanently canceled and replaced by the balanced system.)

Owens Corning ProEdge Hip & Ridge Shingles: Provides an architectural, high-profile look while delivering enhanced system performance and 130 mph wind resistance on all hips and ridges.

CLEAR UV-RATED SEALANT:
High-performance, UV-rated sealant applied on all pipe jacks and penetration locations. This superior material is designed for long-term maintenance, significantly reducing the need for pipe jack replacement.

ROOF CEMENT:
Asphalt-based cement used only as a supplemental adhesive or sealant for cut shingle edges and manufacturer-specified locations.

SITE LOGISTICS & FINAL CLEANUP:
Comprehensive magnetic sweep of the entire property, end-of-shift debris removal, and final site inspection to ensure no waste remains.

CERTIFICATE OF COMPLETION:
Issued upon final inspection, this Certificate formally confirms project completion and activates all manufacturer and workmanship warranties.

COMPLIMENTARY MAINTENANCE INSPECTIONS:
A complimentary, scheduled system check provided upon request. Schedule: Annual for the first two years, then biennial (every other year) until year 15, then annual thereafter. Purpose: Focuses on proactive maintenance, checking all pipe seals and flashing integrity, and identifying any maintenance requirements or debris to ensure system longevity.

NOTE ON SUBSTITUTIONS: RHIVE Construction may substitute materials with those of the same kind and quality only if the specified material is unavailable and will ensure all manufacturer warranty requirements remain fully intact.

This premium upgrade includes all the core guarantees and certified installation standards of the Performance Roofing Package, transforming your home’s appearance by replacing the standard components with a complete, integrated GAF Designer system. This package is engineered for homeowners seeking unparalleled curb appeal and a stunning, dimensional aesthetic.
Primary Advantages of this Upgrade:
Stunning Dimensional Appearance: The GAF Woodland® shingles are crafted with a custom, heavy-duty profile that mimics the look of classic wood shake, delivering exceptional visual texture and a dramatic upgrade to your home's curb appeal.
Total System Integrity: By utilizing GAF's full line of integrated components—from the underlayment to the ridge cap—you receive a complete roofing system where every part is specifically engineered to work together for peak performance.
Enhanced GAF System Plus® Warranty: This fully integrated GAF system qualifies for the superior System Plus® Limited Warranty, providing 50 years of non-prorated material protection and 10 years of workmanship coverage.

DUMPSTER & WASTE MANAGEMENT:
Cost included for disposal of all estimated layers, debris, and waste. Includes placement and management of dumpster and ground tarps.

TEAR-OFF:
Full removal of all existing roofing material down to the decking. Any additional layers will be photographed and communicated to the homeowner for unmatched transparency.

PARAPET WALL PREP & MEMBRANE REMOVAL: Full removal of the existing membrane and flashing from parapet walls down to the nailer, followed by inspection for substrate moisture.

COPING CAP (DETACH & RESET): Existing metal coping caps are carefully detached, preserved, and then re-secured after the new TPO flashing is complete to ensure a weather-tight seal. (SEE UPGRADE OPTIONS FOR NEW COPING CAP MATERIAL OPTIONS)

DECKING INSPECTION:
Certification inspection of all roof surface decking. Non-compliant decking quoted with project owner approval.

DECKING:
Up to 100 sq ft of 7/16 OSB Decking replacement included. Metal decking is quoted as required

WOOD NAILER INSTALLATION: New wood nailers are installed or replaced at the perimeter to provide a structural attachment point. Pressure-treated lumber is used when insulation is thicker than $1/4 \text{ in}$; OSB is used for thinner (fanfold) systems.

INSULATION (MANDATORY): Polyisocyanurate (Polyiso) insulation is provided to achieve the specified R-Value (e.g., R-25, R-30, etc.) for energy efficiency and compliance. (¼” Fanfold EPS) is used as a protective barrier/leveling course on systems that do not require external R-value.


TAPERED INSULATION INSTALLATION: Pre-fabricated tapered polyiso insulation is installed around curbs, drains, and scuppers to promote positive drainage and eliminate standing water.

FASTENERS & PLATES: GAF Drill-Tec™ #15 XHD screws are used throughout. Standard plates secure the insulation and cover board. Barbed plates are used to secure the TPO membrane, ensuring maximum wind uplift resistance for the entire system.
PERIMETER & EAVE METAL: GAF-specified clad metal drip edge is installed at all roof eaves, mechanically secured into the perimeter wood nailer for a complete, watertight edge termination.
FLASHING (VERTICAL & ROOF-TO-WALL): Vertical membrane flashing on parapets is secured with LV50 Quick Spray adhesive. The roof-to-wall transition is taken behind existing siding and extends to the height required by GAF specifications to prevent water ingress.
TPO HEAT WELDING & DETAIL: All membrane seams are fusion heat-welded to create a monolithic, watertight bond. Daily, continuous, test welds documented to ensure 100% adherence to manufacturer specifications.
PIPE, CURB, & CUSTOM DETAILS: All curbs are fully wrapped with membrane. Standard penetrations use pre-molded GAF universal corners and pipe boots. For immovable units (no D&R), flashing is mechanically secured at the base of the equipment per GAF specifications, and the seal detail is engineered to meet vertical termination requirements without removal.
TERMINATION BAR & COUNTER FLASHING: Heavy-gauge metal termination bar mechanically secures the membrane at the top of vertical surfaces. It is paired with counter flashing to protect the seal from water infiltration.
DRAINS & SCUPPERS: Existing drains and scuppers are flashed with specialized TPO boot details. Custom-built metal scuppers and new clamping ring drains are installed as needed to ensure proper internal drainage.
COVER BOARD (DensDeck®): Conditional Inclusion
A high-density fiberglass-mat gypsum cover board is installed over the insulation to provide a smooth, abuse-resistant substrate for the TPO membrane and enhance the system's fire rating (Included only when specified in the quote scope).

Strategic Structural Protection and Warranty Compliance. We safely cut and clear all vegetation (branches, vines, brush) that touches or overhangs your roof, fascia, or gutters. This service is a critical, proactive measure that stops the most common causes of premature roof failure.
The RHIVE Clearance Zone
We achieve system defense through two core actions:
12-Inch Compliance Zone: We create a minimum 12-inch clearance zone by trimming intrusive vegetation. This is the structural standard necessary to protect your roof membrane from physical abrasion, prevent moisture retention, and keep your manufacturer warranty fully intact across all major brands.
Debris & Water Stop: Eliminating overhang directly stops the continuous drop of leaves, pine needles, and sticks onto your roof, preventing debris accumulation that traps water and accelerates shingle degradation.
Execution Blueprint
This service is focused purely on cutting and structural clearance.
VEGETATION REMOVAL: Precise removal of tree limbs, brush, and vines to achieve the 12-Inch Compliance Zone.
DOCUMENTATION INSPECTION: We will document the condition of the roof surface underneath any previously obscured vegetation. If underlying damage (abrasion, rot, or moisture) is found, the project owner will be updated with clear photo evidence before any repair work is estimated.
DEBRIS DISPOSAL: All cut vegetation, branches, and large trimming debris are responsibly disposed of.

Essential Flow Restoration. This service is engineered to restore the full, intended water flow capacity of your gutter and downspout system. We eliminate blockages that cause overflow, preventing water damage to your fascia and foundation. This is a focused maintenance service to ensure your existing system drains effectively.
The RHIVE Clear-Flow Protocol (Debris Only)
Service Scope: Focused solely on the internal removal of accumulated debris to restore free flow. This service does not include gutter resealing, realignment, or component repair.
Debris Eviction: Comprehensive manual removal of all leaves, sticks, granular sediment, and other organic waste from the interior of all gutter troughs and horizontal sections.
Downspout Flushing: Targeted water flushing or air pressure is applied to all downspouts to quickly clear internal blockages and verify 100% vertical flow capacity.
Execution Blueprint
GUTTER INSPECTION & PERFORMANCE REPORT: We inspect the gutter system for proper slope and alignment, ensuring water channels correctly and doesn't stand (which causes corrosion). We also examine connections, fasteners, and downspout outlets. We will document and recommend any required resealing, realignment, or structural repairs needed to keep your gutter system operating properly and protect your structure long-term.

Proactive Leak Defense Extension. This targeted maintenance service is designed to extend the service life of your existing pipe flashings (pipe jacks/vents). We clean, inspect, and apply our superior clear UV-rated sealant to the vulnerable collar seal, eliminating minor degradation and postponing the need for a full, replacement.
The RHIVE Quantum Maintenance Protocol
Service Scope: Focused solely on the cleaning, inspection, and resealing of the existing pipe jack components. This service does not include removal, replacement, or shingle repair.
Surface Preparation: Thorough cleaning and surface prep of the existing pipe boot and pipe to ensure maximum adherence of the new sealant. We remove old, cracked, or degraded sealants to expose a clean bonding surface.
Corrosion Mitigation: For existing metal jacks, any minor surface oxidation is treated to stabilize the component before applying the new sealant.
Execution Blueprint
UV-Rated Sealant Application: Application of a high-performance, Clear UV-Rated Sealant around the base of the pipe collar. This flexible material creates a new, long-term, weather-tight barrier designed specifically to resist breakdown from UV rays and thermal cycling.
System Check: Final inspection to ensure a smooth, contiguous seal has been achieved and to document the condition of the pipe jack and surrounding shingle field.

Item: EMERGENCY MEMBRANE REPAIR | IMMEDIATE LEAK CONTAINMENT (FLAT ROOFS)
Description:
Immediate Defense: Critical Membrane Containment. This is a rapid-response service engineered to provide an immediate, temporary, and robust seal against water infiltration caused by breaches in flat roof membranes (TPO, PVC, etc.). We use specialized membrane patches, adhesives, or sealants to protect the structural decking and interior until permanent repairs can be scheduled.
The RHIVE Quantum Rapid-Response Protocol
Service Scope: Focused entirely on immediate, surgical repair and containment of a localized membrane breach. This service provides a temporary, high-performance waterproof seal and does not include full membrane replacement or comprehensive system repair.
Response Goal: To eliminate active water intrusion at the point of failure and protect the underlying structure and building contents.
Execution Blueprint: Multi-Tiered Containment
We deploy the optimal containment method based on the membrane condition and compatibility:
Method 1: Fusion Heat-Welded Patch
Process: A compatible membrane patch is placed over the compromised area. The failing membrane is cleaned and conditioned to be weldable, and the patch is fusion heat-welded to the existing system, creating the industry's highest standard temporary bond.
Method 2: Adhered Patch with Weld Perimeter
Process: A membrane patch is fully adhered to the failing area using a commercial adhesive (e.g., LV50 Quick Spray). The perimeter of the adhered patch is then fully heat-welded to create an additional layer of perimeter defense, ensuring maximum wind and water resistance.
Method 3: Roll-On Sealant
Process: For aged membranes that are not conducive to welding, the area is thoroughly cleaned and conditioned. A liquid flashing or specialized flexible roof sealant is then rolled onto the compromised area, creating a seamless, fully adhered, waterproof layer of defense.
Minimal Footprint: The repair is limited to the immediate area required to stop the leak, ensuring minimal disruption to the existing field of the roof.

Proactive Maintenance for Peak Performance & Longevity. This is a critical maintenance service engineered to clear all organic and inorganic debris from the roof surface. By systematically removing materials that trap moisture, accelerate degradation, and obstruct drainage, we restore your roof's designed performance, prevent premature failure, and ensure compliance with manufacturer warranty requirements.

The RHIVE System Integrity Protocol
Warranty Compliance: The accumulation of debris is a leading cause of premature roof failure and can void manufacturer warranties. This service is a key step in protecting your investment and ensuring long-term system coverage.
Pitched Roof Integrity (Asphalt Shingles): We eliminate debris that acts like a sponge, holding moisture against shingles and causing algae growth, granule loss, and underlying rot. Our process clears critical water-shedding channels, especially in valleys, to prevent water damming and protect the roof's structural integrity.
Flat Roof Drainage (Commercial Membranes): We ensure positive drainage by clearing the entire field of the roof, with specific focus on drains, scuppers, and crickets. This prevents ponding water—the primary adversary of flat roofs—which leads to membrane degradation, seam failure, and structural load issues.
Surface-Safe Methodology: All removal is performed using non-abrasive, manufacturer-approved methods (such as controlled air blowers and soft-bristled tools) to clear debris without damaging shingle granules or compromising the integrity of the roof membrane.

Execution Blueprint
SAFETY & ASSESSMENT: We begin by establishing a safe work zone and assessing the roof for any pre-existing conditions or hazards.
BULK DEBRIS REMOVAL: All loose debris—including leaves, branches, dirt, and refuse—is systematically removed from the entire roof field. On pitched roofs, we work from the ridge down to ensure a complete clean.
DETAIL CLEANING OF CRITICAL AREAS: We perform precision cleaning of all crucial drainage paths and vulnerable areas, including roof valleys, skylight perimeters, pipe penetrations, HVAC curbs, drains, and scuppers.
DRAINAGE PATH VERIFICATION: After the roof surface is clear, we verify that gutters and downspout openings at the roofline are unobstructed to ensure a clear drainage path from the roof to the ground.
FINAL INSPECTION & DISPOSAL: A final inspection confirms a clear roof. All resulting debris is then managed with care to ensure a clean site upon completion.

Preservation of Existing Components with an Enhanced Reinstallation. This service is performed when the existing metal coping caps are in good, serviceable condition. It includes the careful preservation of the original caps and a professional reinstallation that provides a more durable, weather-resistant attachment while maintaining the building's clean exterior aesthetic.
Execution Blueprint:
Careful Detachment: The existing coping caps are carefully detached from the parapet wall to minimize any bending or damage.
On-Site Preservation: The coping sections are staged and preserved securely on-site during primary roofing operations.
Professional Reinstallation with Concealed Fastening: After the new roof membrane is complete, the original coping caps are reinstalled. To preserve the clean exterior appearance, the caps are secured by driving new, specialized fasteners with integrated neoprene washers through the interior leg (roof side) of the coping cap. The neoprene washer compresses against the metal to create a durable, watertight seal at each penetration.
Final Sealing: All joints between coping sections are sealed with a new application of high-performance, UV-stable sealant. The new interior fastener heads are also sealed to provide redundant, long-term weather protection.
